_comment: Deployments-API payload for the daily AI brief's Managed Agents scheduled deployment.
Source-of-truth per docs/adr/0006 FR-16/AC-15 — this file is what defines the
schedule and which self-hosted environment/agent it targets. It is NOT applied
automatically by this CDK stack; create/update the deployment via the Deployments
API or Claude Console using this file's values (see deploy/managed-agent/README.md).
Built against beta header: managed-agents-2026-04-01 (PRD FR-2). If Anthropic
changes this API surface, this file's shape may need to change too — the
deployment must fail loudly, not silently skip, if the platform contract changes
_deployment_history: depl_01GfuYeqwuDJ3q968CpTUUDe (archived 2026-07-07): the in-VM audio_email.py delivery path. Superseded by the ADR-0015 production delivery decoupling cut-over -- production DELIVERY (Polly narration, SES send, S3 archival, subscriber fan-out, and deterministic Markdown->HTML) moved OFF the in-VM audio_email.py path onto the standalone deploy/delivery/ boundary. The new deployment's initial_prompt runs delivery_client.py (POST /deliver with the four content artifacts) instead of audio_email.py, and MicroVmExecutionRole was stripped of all AWS delivery IAM via the deliveryDecoupled=true CDK context (env-key + logs + the two delivery-secret reads only -- FR-1/AC-1). Validated owner-only on the stripped role first; confirmed exactly one active cron deployment before this note; the new deployment below was confirmed active first.

depl_0132ARBCdsSRh6hxocbbW7ac (archived 2026-07-04): superseded when the reader-feedback feature added FEEDBACK_TOKEN_SECRET_ARN/FEEDBACK_BASE_URL to the initial_prompt's env exports -- deployments are immutable (PATCH/PUT return 405; only GET/HEAD/POST/DELETE are allowed on /v1/deployments/{id}), so an update means POST a new deployment (same agent/environment/schedule, revised initial_events) then POST .../archive on the old one (DELETE on an active deployment 404s; archiving is the actual mechanism despite DELETE appearing in the Allow header).

depl_018VbiHQAKzmnw2VDAZNtbe6 (archived 2026-07-04): superseded once DNS for feedback.mschweier.com was cut over -- flipped FEEDBACK_BASE_URL from the temporary CloudFront default domain to the real subdomain.

depl_01DT5kh3kr4D4168CMqsQiXw (archived 2026-07-04): superseded to pick up `export ENABLE_SUBSCRIBER_FANOUT=1` in the initial_prompt -- the eval-harness security fix flipped audio_email.py's subscriber fan-out gate from an opt-out SKIP_SUBSCRIBER_FANOUT (defaulted fan-out ON if unset) to an opt-in ENABLE_SUBSCRIBER_FANOUT (defaults fan-out OFF unless explicitly asserted); this deployment predated that change and needed the new export added to keep fanning out to real subscribers. Confirmed archived (upcoming_runs_at: []) before this note was written; the new deployment below was confirmed active first.

agent._comment: References the agent definition created via the Claude Console/API (docs/adr/0007). The skill (deploy/managed-agent/skills/daily-ai-brief/) is a verified verbatim port of the real daily-ai-brief skill, incl. its real sources.md; it deliberately does NOT touch Polly/SES/HTML/delivery. Post-ADR-0015 decoupling, this initial_prompt is the content-generation wrapper ONLY: it reads recent priors (delivery_client.py read-recent-briefs -> GET /recent-briefs), invokes the skill for research/writing/listening-script, then hands the four content artifacts (brief markdown + listening script + candidates + source-usage) to the standalone deploy/delivery/ boundary via delivery_client.py (POST /deliver). It does NOT run audio_email.py and the microVM role holds NO AWS delivery IAM -- HTML derivation, Polly narration, SES send, subscriber fan-out, and S3 archival all happen on the delivery side (FR-1/AC-1).
agent.initial_prompt: 0) Before researching: export DELIVERY_BASE_URL=https://6nbe4wsng6.execute-api.us-east-1.amazonaws.com and RECENT_BRIEFS_SIGNING_SECRET_ARN=arn:aws:secretsmanager:us-east-1:740353583786:secret:daily-ai-brief/recent-briefs-read-bearer-secret-l8UJaq, then run `python3.13 /opt/pipeline/delivery_client.py read-recent-briefs`. This reads the recent-briefs signing key from Secrets Manager via the microVM's own role, mints a short-lived signed token, calls GET /recent-briefs on the decoupled delivery boundary, and writes each returned prior brief to its own /workspace/AI Brief - <its own actual date>.md (none on a first run, fewer than 3 early on -- both fine; it degrades gracefully to no priors on any read failure and never aborts the run). 1) Invoke the daily-ai-brief skill to research, write, and validate today's brief and produce the listening script, per the skill's own output contract: the skill's Step 1 finds the files from step 0 via its normal WORKING_FOLDER search to avoid repeating stories and correctly label follow-ups by each file's actual date. It writes today's brief to /workspace/AI Brief - YYYY-MM-DD.md (today's date), saves the listening script explicitly to /workspace/listening-script.txt, and writes its additive candidates.json and source-usage.json to /workspace/. 2) Run the delivery step: `python3.13 /opt/pipeline/delivery_client.py`, after exporting BRIEF_MARKDOWN_PATH (today's actual dated output file from step 1), LISTENING_SCRIPT_PATH=/workspace/listening-script.txt, CANDIDATES_PATH=/workspace/candidates.json, SOURCE_USAGE_PATH=/workspace/source-usage.json, EMAIL_SUBJECT='Daily AI Brief — <today, DD.MM.YYYY>', PIPELINE_TIMEZONE=Europe/Berlin (must match this deployment's schedule timezone -- it determines both today's archive date and how 'today' resolves for recent priors), DELIVERY_BASE_URL=https://6nbe4wsng6.execute-api.us-east-1.amazonaws.com, DELIVERY_BEARER_SECRET_ARN=arn:aws:secretsmanager:us-east-1:740353583786:secret:daily-ai-brief/delivery-bearer-secret-AzvpYF, and ENABLE_SUBSCRIBER_FANOUT=1. This client reads the POST /deliver bearer from Secrets Manager via the microVM role, POSTs the four content artifacts (brief markdown + listening script + candidates + source-usage) to the decoupled delivery boundary -- which deterministically derives the inbox HTML, synthesizes the narrated MP3 (async Polly, text-only fail-safe), sends, and archives all four to S3 briefs/<date>/ -- and polls to completion, failing LOUDLY (non-zero exit) on any delivery failure so the brief is never silently dropped. Do NOT convert Markdown to HTML yourself and do NOT run audio_email.py -- HTML derivation and all AWS delivery now happen on the delivery side. PRODUCTION RUN: ENABLE_SUBSCRIBER_FANOUT=1 is exported above, so the delivery side sends the owner's copy to mail@mschweier.com AND fans out one personalized copy (with per-subscriber feedback + unsubscribe links) to every confirmed subscriber.
skills: daily-ai-brief (skill_01H2qu83NwnJ5zqcbrqsCcJ6, set on the agent itself -- not re-specified per-deployment)

environment._comment: The self_hosted environment id created via the Claude Console after the CDK stack (ManagedAgentSandboxStack) is deployed and its webhook is registered. See README step 2.

schedule._comment: Matches today's weekday 6:07 AM local run (docs/adr/0006). Confirmed with the owner 2026-07-03: actual local timezone is Europe/Berlin (the ADR's original America/Los_Angeles was an unconfirmed placeholder, corrected before deploy).

_manual_steps_not_encoded_here: Creating the agent (agent_id) and the self_hosted environment (environment_id) via the Claude Console/API.
Registering deploy/managed-agent's WebhookUrl stack output with Anthropic for session.status_run_started.
Generating and populating the environment key + webhook signing secret into the two Secrets Manager secrets this CDK stack creates empty.
Building and pushing the microVM container image (deploy/managed-agent/microvm/) via the reference implementation's build-image.sh-equivalent flow.
Actually calling the Deployments API (or Console) with this file's schedule/agent/environment values to create or update the live scheduled deployment.
See deploy/managed-agent/README.md for the full, ordered runbook.
